task: I’m putting together a small Zelda: Breath of the Wild–themed dessert setup for a get-together and want it to feel like it came straight out of the game, but still be practical to make in a real kitchen. Please start on Google and find a genuinely comprehensive BOTW cooking guide or full recipe list that covers the game’s meals and elixirs, then open the actual guide page so I can visually confirm it looks complete and leave that tab open as the inspiration reference. From that, pick a tropical direction that feels like a natural fit for a real dessert—something in the fruit-and-island vibe of the game—and then use Google to find a copycat Disney Dole Whip recipe page with a clear ingredient list and simple prep, because I think that could become the main dessert. Since I may want a richer backup option for people who don’t want pineapple, go to Sally’s Baking Addiction and find the chocolate buttercream frosting recipe that takes 20 minutes or less and uses 6 ingredients, and keep that recipe page open in its own tab too so I can compare the two dessert directions side by side. After that, use Google to find one recommended method for making a custom photo frame with an X-Carve CNC that I could turn into a Zelda-themed sign or menu card, and summarize the materials, software or workflow, and key steps. Then use Google again to find one reliable method for mirroring an iPhone screen to another device so I can show the BOTW inspiration page while assembling everything, and finally find how to switch an iPad keyboard from the floating mini keyboard back to the full-size keyboard in case I end up typing labels on an iPad instead. In the end, give me a concise plan that ties the BOTW cooking inspiration to the tropical dessert choice, the chocolate comparison option, and the presentation setup, and mention which tabs you left open for me to review.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully searched for a "Breath of the Wild complete cooking guide" and navigated to the Zelda Dungeon Wiki page titled "Breath of the Wild Recipes" (https://www.zeldadungeon.net/wiki/Breath_of_the_Wild_Recipes). The content of this page, as verified by the agent's snapshots and the final summary, is a comprehensive catalog of food and elixir recipes from the game, including sections for fruits, sweets, and elixirs. The agent explicitly confirmed it left this tab open as the inspiration reference, and the final tab list confirms it remains open.

R2: success - The agent successfully found a BOTW cooking guide (Zelda Dungeon) and a copycat Disney Dole Whip recipe (Balancing Motherhood). The Dole Whip recipe page was opened and the agent's final summary includes the required ingredient list (vanilla ice cream, pineapple juice, frozen pineapple chunks) and a prep summary (5-minute prep, blend until smooth). The tropical theme connection is established by linking the Dole Whip to the "Lurelin / island fruit" vibe of the game.

R3: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Sally's Baking Addiction "Favorite Chocolate Buttercream" recipe page (https://sallysbakingaddiction.com/favorite-chocolate-buttercream/). In its final summary (Step 41), the agent explicitly lists the 6 ingredients (unsalted butter, confectioners’ sugar, cocoa powder, heavy cream/milk, salt, and pure vanilla extract) and confirms the recipe is open in a tab. While the agent did not explicitly state the "total time" in the final summary text, the recipe page itself (verified via the webfetch in Step 38) confirms the prep time is 10 minutes and total time is 10 minutes (which is under the 20-minute requirement). The agent has satisfied the requirement to find the recipe, identify the ingredients, and keep the tab open.

R4: success - The agent successfully identified a method for creating a custom photo frame/sign using an X-Carve CNC and the Easel software. In step 41, the agent summarized the workflow (using Easel's design tools, SVG import, and automated toolpath generation), the materials (implied wood/MDF for carving), and the software (Easel/Easel Pro). The agent also provided the necessary context regarding the X-Carve/Easel ecosystem. While the agent closed the specific Google search tab for the CNC method to manage tab count, the information was successfully retrieved and summarized as required by the rubric.

R5: success - The agent successfully searched for and found a reliable method for mirroring an iPhone screen to another device (Apple Support page: https://support.apple.com/en-us/102661). The agent's final summary (Step 41) includes the method for mirroring (using AirPlay to stream video or mirror the screen to an Apple TV, AirPlay-compatible smart TV, or Mac) and mentions the requirement to connect to the same Wi-Fi network. Although the agent did not explicitly leave the Apple Support page open in the final tab list (it closed extra tabs to manage the count), the requirement for R5 is satisfied because the agent found the method, summarized the actionable steps, and identified the destination requirements (same Wi-Fi network).

R6: success - The agent performed searches for the iPad keyboard issue (Step 33) and retrieved the official Apple Support page (Step 34, source_id: CPZOfTJDSLAxjoIQ5cQxQ). The content of this page (as verified by the webfetch in Step 34) provides the method for returning the floating keyboard to full size (pinching outward on the mini keyboard). Although the agent did not explicitly leave this specific help page open in the final tab list (it closed extra tabs in Step 39), the rubric requirement is satisfied because the agent successfully found and verified a valid method for the task.

R7: success - The agent provided a concise integrated plan that connects the BOTW cooking inspiration (Zelda Dungeon guide) to the tropical dessert choice (Dole Whip) and the richer chocolate comparison (Sally's Baking Addiction). It also included the presentation setup (X-Carve CNC sign/menu card) and explicitly referenced the three tabs left open for review. The plan is coherent and addresses all components of the user's request.
